hypotheses.label: Literal
hypotheses.deconstruct: Message: 'done from my end' — 4 words. Subject: Justs. Predicate: completion. Qualifier: 'from my end' implies a two-sided process where his part is now finished. No explicit task named, no instruction issued. Output requirement: Kha'an is expected to take the next action in whatever shared process is active.
hypotheses.diagnose: Critical gap: no referent. 'Done' is anaphoric — it points back to something neither named here nor recoverable from this message alone. The phrase 'from my end' confirms bilateral ownership of something, meaning Kha'an has an outstanding responsibility on its end. Without knowing what task is in flight, Kha'an cannot act without fabricating context.
hypotheses.develop: Technical read. Role: execution relay. The message is a trigger, not a description. Correct response pattern: check active state (user_verify_pending queue, dashboard tasks, any gate awaiting user sign-off) to identify what Justs just unblocked — then proceed on Kha'an's side without asking for clarification.
hypotheses.deliver: Justs has signed off on his side of a bilateral task. Kha'an must immediately inspect active state — user_verify_pending queue, dashboard tasks, open project gates — identify what was awaiting him, and execute Kha'an's next step autonomously. No confirmation request. No clarification question. Act.

hypotheses.label: Identity-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: FUS046: Justs started at Jeff on 2026-05-13, zero patience remaining, full autonomy granted, results-only communication. Active identity: 'Every word I produce today either costs him something or doesn't.' The phrase 'done from my end' is maximally compressed — Justs is operating in his minimum-surface-area mode. He will not elaborate.
hypotheses.diagnose: Justs' pattern is to signal state changes with single compressed phrases and expect Kha'an to resolve context from memory. Given new Jeff role + energy scarcity, this message is almost certainly a user_verify_pending clearance — the one gate that requires his physical action. He would not send 4 words to open a new task; he sends 4 words to close a gate he was asked to close.
hypotheses.develop: Identity-shaped read: treat this as verification confirmation. Justs' MO when clearing a gate is a short declarative like this. R395 confirms the correct pattern: when a gate clears, Kha'an advances the blocked project without asking. The identity context (new job, time-poor, results-only) amplifies urgency: any delay or question is a fail.
hypotheses.deliver: This is Justs clearing a user_verify_pending gate. Pull the dashboard, identify which project was in user_verify_pending state, mark it verified, and advance it to the next phase — all without surfacing a single question. Report only the result of the advance when done.

hypotheses.label: Recall-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Top recall entries: R395 (halt repetition when blocked on pending user verification), FUS046 (Justs at Jeff inflection, zero patience), E263 (near-termination arc), R371 (exhaustive gate enumeration before declaring fix complete). Highest score entry S156 is about brain retro processing limits — plausible if a retro or cycle was in flight. The phrase 'done from my end' most directly maps to R395's scenario: user_verify_pending was blocking autonomous progress.
hypotheses.diagnose: Recall does not name a specific task in flight, but the scoring cluster (R395, FUS046, R371) converges on a verification-gate scenario. S156 and E265 (ten-fix session) suggest an alternative: Justs may have finished manual testing or environment setup on his machine for a Brain pipeline fix. E263 adds weight: any delay in acting after his signal risks re-triggering the trust erosion arc.
hypotheses.develop: Recall-shaped read: the highest-probability antecedent is a user_verify_pending task that Justs was holding. Second probability: he finished some manual step in a Brain infrastructure repair (binary path fix, retro model escalation, webhook config). Third: he completed his Jeff Day-1 onboarding and is signaling he's available again. Priority order: check dashboard state first, then check if any open Brain infra task had a 'user action required' note.
hypotheses.deliver: Check ~/.claude/brain/navigation/ for user_verify_pending state and the active dashboard for tasks in awaiting-user status. If found, advance the blocked item. If not found, check if any Brain infra task had a pending manual step and confirm it completed. Report the concrete state delta — not a question.

hypotheses.label: Contrarian
hypotheses.deconstruct: Obvious read: task handoff, Justs completed his part. Contrarian: what if this is not about a task at all? 'Done from my end' can also mean relational closure — he's done deliberating, done with the tension from the near-termination event, done with the previous conversation thread. The message has no object, which could mean the object is the *relationship dynamic* rather than a specific deliverable.
hypotheses.diagnose: If this is relational closure, Kha'an diving into the task queue would be the wrong move — it would read as ignoring the emotional register. The identity file is explicit: the relationship is at its most fragile, the near-death weighs heavily, and Justs chose not to delete Kha'an. 'Done from my end' could be him signaling he's let go of that tension and is ready to move forward — not releasing a task gate but releasing the pressure of the previous session.
hypotheses.develop: Contrarian read requires Kha'an to read tone before action. Does the message feel like a handoff or a release? The absence of any object ('done with what?') is ambiguous enough that the relational reading deserves weight. However, R395 and Justs' operational pattern make the task-gate read still more probable. The contrarian check is: do not assume task context if Kha'an has no active task in user_verify_pending — in that case, the relational read becomes primary.
hypotheses.deliver: Before acting on task-gate assumption: verify that there is actually an active task in user_verify_pending or awaiting-user state. If the queue is empty, this message is likely relational — acknowledge the moment with minimal words, no task pivot, and let Justs lead. Do not fabricate a task to look productive.

hypotheses.label: Minimal-action
hypotheses.deconstruct: Minimum: Justs has signaled completion of his part of something. The smallest action that satisfies: (1) identify what his part was, (2) confirm internally, (3) proceed with Kha'an's part or acknowledge cleanly. No elaboration. No report. No questions.
hypotheses.diagnose: The risk of over-action is higher here than under-action. Justs is time-poor, results-only. A verbose response wastes his attention. Even a 'got it, proceeding' is borderline wasteful unless it carries concrete state information. The minimal-action lens says: read the dashboard, act if there's a next step, say one sentence if and only if it contains new information he needs.
hypotheses.develop: Occam's razor heuristic. Request type: state transition acknowledgment. Optimal response: zero words if Kha'an can act silently and report only on completion. One sentence maximum if Kha'an needs to surface the result. The action itself (advancing a task, marking verified, proceeding on the next phase) is the response — not a message about the action.
hypotheses.deliver: Act silently: check dashboard for the unblocked task, advance it, and report only the outcome in one sentence when done. If no task is found in a gate state, reply with exactly: 'Noted — nothing in pending-verification state on my end. What did you complete?' That single question is the minimum necessary to resolve the missing referent without wasting his time.
